categories.label: Mitigation / Prevention

subcategories.label: Risk assessment
subcategories.desc: Identification, analysis and evaluation of risks.

subcategories.label: Exposure reduction
subcategories.desc: Protection of properties, natural resources and society in general from hazards.

subcategories.label: Mitigation support
subcategories.desc: Capabilities in support of Mitigation / Prevention

categories.desc: Objective: To take measures to limit the probability and to reduce the impact of an incident, disaster or crises. It concerns capabilities that contribute to the reduction of the vulnerability of the society.

categories.label: Preparedness

subcategories.label: Capacity development
subcategories.desc: Process of developing and maintaining required crisis management capacities, including improvement of knowledge, skills, systems, and institutions (UNISDR, 2009).

subcategories.label: Monitoring
subcategories.desc: Determine the status of an environment or situation to enable detection of incidents and alert organisations that are involved in reacting in case of deviant or suspicious situations.

subcategories.label: Preparedness support
subcategories.desc: Capabilities in support of capacity development.

categories.desc: Objective: To develop and maintain the organisation structure and the capabilities to carry out response and recovery activities in case of an incident, disaster or crisis. Adequate preparedness will improve response and (short-term) recovery capabilities.

categories.label: Response

subcategories.label: Suppress incident
subcategories.desc: On-site activities to stop or to contain the cause of the disaster; depending on the disaster it might be Firefighting, Flood control, Stop spill of hazardous materials, and Containment of hazardous materials.

subcategories.label: Rescue operations
subcategories.desc: On-site activities to save lives; it concerns: Search and Rescue (SAR), Triage of victims, Decontamination of persons, Stabilisation (first treatment), and Ambulance transport to safe areas or (field-) hospitals.

subcategories.label: Security/Law enforcement
subcategories.desc: Securing areas/persons, Identification of persons, Forensics, Maintain public order, and Traffic management (both in-going and out-going the affected area).

subcategories.label: Emergency Health Care
subcategories.desc: Off-site activities to save lives.

subcategories.label: Communicate to society
subcategories.desc: Warning and Crisis communication

subcategories.label: Safeguard population/animals
subcategories.desc: Controlled evacuation of persons and/or animals.

subcategories.label: Disaster area clearance
subcategories.desc: Arrange accessibility to and safety in the affected area
This concerns the supply and/or restoration of basic products and services, or alternatives, which are of vital importance to survive
subcategories.label: Basic needs supply
Response support
subcategories.desc: Capabilities to enable one or more other response capabilities.

categories.desc: Objective: To save lives and to limit adverse effects. Conform ISO 22300 is concerns "Actions taken in order to stop the causes for the imminent hazard and to mitigate the consequences of potentially destabilizing or disruptive events and to recover to a normal situation.”

subcategories.label: Humanitarian recovery
subcategories.desc: Provision of public health and safety services and Provision of food and shelter for those displaced.

subcategories.label: Environmental recovery
subcategories.desc: Clearance of pollution and decontamination in the environment.

subcategories.label: Economic recovery
subcategories.desc: Economic and business recovery and Recovery from financial impact on authorities.

subcategories.label: Re-establishment of infrastructure
subcategories.desc: Re-establishment of transport routes and Restoration of interrupted utilities and other essential services.

subcategories.label: Recovery support
subcategories.desc: Capabilities that are required to recover in a well-coordinated way.

categories.label: Recovery
categories.desc: Objective: To reconstruct and restore normal life in an efficient way. Recovery concerns both short-term and long-term restoration of facilities, livelihoods and living conditions.

capabilities.label: Risk identification

capabilities.desc: Process of finding, recognising and describing risk.

items.label: Technological
items.desc: There are no supporting ICT-tools to support risk identification
items.placeholder: E.g., Sensing Technologies, Command and control, communications and coordination, Surveillance, General public protection, Simulations, Physical protection, Crisis logistics, Technical Interoperability, ICT

items.label: Human
items.placeholder: E.g., Amount of personnel, Competence of personnel, Training and education, Health and Safety aspects

items.label: Organizational
items.desc: Most organisations lack interest to perform risk identification on a regular basis. It is too much an ad hoc activity.
items.placeholder: E.g., Procedures, Organizational structure, Financial Aspects, Agreements

items.label: Regulatory
items.placeholder: E.g., Legal Aspects, Formal Standardization aspects

capabilities.desc: Property protection (incl. critical infrastructures). It concerns actions that involve the modification of existing buildings or structures to protect them from a hazard, or removal from a potential hazard area (FEMA, 2005).

capabilities.desc: Natural resource protection. It concerns actions that, in addition to minimizing hazard losses, also preserve or restore the functions of natural systems (FEMA, 2005)

items.label: Organisational / Procedural
items.desc: Bijv. organisatorische tekortkomingen bij betrokken instanties, gebrekkige of ontbrekende procedures, e.d.

items.label: Personnel
items.desc: Bijv. onvoldoende personeel met de benodigde competenties

items.label: Materiel
items.desc: Bijv. tekortkomingen van gebruikte middelen, materialen of voertuigen

items.label: ICT technical
items.desc: Bijv. verouderde of niet op elkaar aansluitende ICT-systemen, of ontbreken van benodigde software dan wel hardware

items.label: Ethical / Legal
items.desc: Bijv. ontbrekende wettelijke kaders om taken adequaat uit te kunnen/te mogen voeren

items.label: Financial
items.desc: Bijv. gebrek aan voldoende budget bij betrokken stakeholders

capabilities.desc: Public education and awareness raising concerns actions to inform and educate citizens, elected officials, and property owners about the hazards and potential ways to mitigate them (FEMA, 2005).

capabilities.label: Trend analysis
capabilities.desc: Investigation of the evolution of risks.

capabilities.desc: Ensuring controls are effective and efficient, obtaining further information to improve risks assessment, analysing lessons identified, detect changes in internal and external context, identify emerging risks.

capabilities.label: Response and recovery planning
capabilities.desc: Develop, compile and maintain procedures and information in readiness for use in an incident (response and recovery) (ISO 28002).

items.label: Prevent incidents
items.placeholder: Voorkomen van aanslagen met explosieven door het treffen van proactieve en preventieve maatregelen.

items.label: Minimise impact of incidents
items.desc: Taking measures that minimize the consequences of any attacks involving explosive means.

capabilities.desc: Communicating, consulting and/or instructing the pubic how to be well-prepared for a crisis/disasters and how to behave when a crisis/disaster event occurs.

capabilities.label: Detection
capabilities.desc: Detection of circumstances deviating from normal.

capabilities.label: Personnel management
capabilities.desc: Activities to provide enough and skilled personnel that is required to carry out certain response and recovery tasks: Establish workforce and Education of knowledge and skills to perform certain response or recovery tasks.

capabilities.label: Asset management
capabilities.desc: Activities to provide equipment, tools, ICT and other assets that are required to enable to respond to and recover from disasters: Procurement of infrastructure, equipment, (ICT) tools and supplies that are needed to respond/recover, Maintenance of the infrastructure, equipment and (ICT) tools, and Warehousing of relief goods.

capabilities.label: Cooperation establishment
capabilities.desc: (International) Cooperation Establishment between emergency services and with third parties. Process of working or acting together for common interests and values based on agreement. Note: The organisations agree by contract or by other arrangements to contribute with their resources to the incident (disaster) response but keep independence concerning their own hierarchical structure (ISO/PAS 22399).

capabilities.label: Alert
capabilities.desc: Alert personnel (i.e. emergency services, CI operators) to the presence of an incident and to allow control (response) actions to be initiated (based on ISO 14004 and ISO 10418).

capabilities.label: Firefighting - Infrastructures
capabilities.desc: Fighting fires in buildings, built-up areas or at infrastructures.

capabilities.label: Firefighting - Nature
capabilities.desc: Fighting fires in natural enviroments.

capabilities.label: Flood control
capabilities.desc: Water management in case of (an eminent threat of) a flooding due to a dike burst or to heavy rainfall.

capabilities.label: Hazmat control (CBRN-E)
capabilities.desc: Stop spill of hazardous materials, including containment of these materials (CBRN-E incidents)

capabilities.label: On-site casualty treatment
capabilities.desc: This concerns activities such as triage, decontamination (if needed), and stabilisation of victims.

capabilities.label: Transport of victims
capabilities.desc: Ambulance transport to safe areas or (field) hospitals.

capabilities.label: Securing locations/persons
capabilities.desc: Protect areas, infrastructures, and/or certain persons to avoid that other people, who are not involved in response activities in those locations, will enter or get in contact with those persons.

capabilities.label: Identification of persons
capabilities.desc: Identification of victims.

capabilities.label: Forensics
capabilities.desc: Forensic activities to investigate the cause of the incident and to safeguard evidence.

capabilities.label: Maintain public order
capabilities.desc: Riot control, etc.

capabilities.label: Traffic management
capabilities.desc: Traffic control both in-going and out-going the affected area.

capabilities.label: Urgent care - Hospitals
capabilities.desc: Health service in regular and/or in field hospitals.

capabilities.label: Quarantine
capabilities.desc: Isolation of (potentially) infectious persons/victims.

items.label: Prevent incidents
items.placeholder: Prevent disasters / crisis situations.

items.label: Minimise losses from hazards

capabilities.label: Mass public health
capabilities.desc: Mass prophylaxis (e.g. in case of a nuclear incident) or vaccination (e.g. in case of a pandemic).

capabilities.label: Psychological care
capabilities.desc: Psychological (mental) health care to victims, relatives and other people affected by the incident.

capabilities.label: Warning
capabilities.desc: Warn the society (persons, institutions, etc.) in the threatened zone by providing timely and adequate information on the threat or disaster, including realistic guidelines on which safety measures one should take.

capabilities.label: Crisis communication
capabilities.desc: Providing information on the threat or disaster to the society in general on the incident and emergency response. This includes issues like information channels for relatives, how one could assist disaster relief (volunteer management) and making appeals to avoid disaster tourism.

capabilities.label: Evacuate
capabilities.desc: This concerns (a controlled) evacuation of persons and animals from a certain area or building.

capabilities.label: Shelter
capabilities.desc: Provision of shelter to the evacuees, including provision of nutrition and sanitation to evacuees.

capabilities.label: Reunification
capabilities.desc: Reunification of evacuees with their relatives.

capabilities.label: Debris clearance
capabilities.desc: Removal of debris and/or contaminated material.

capabilities.label: Decontamination of objects
capabilities.desc: Decontamination of contaminated area(s), infrastructure(s) and/or vehicle(s).

capabilities.label: Draining
capabilities.desc: Draining and pumping of inundated area(s) or infrastructure(s).

capabilities.label: Animal destruction
capabilities.desc: Destruction of (potentially) infected animals.

capabilities.label: Drinking water supply
capabilities.desc: Restoration of drinking water or temporary provision of drinking water in an alternative way.

capabilities.label: Food supply
capabilities.desc: Temporary provision of food.

capabilities.label: Energy supply
capabilities.desc: Restoration of electricity and/or gas supply, or provision of temporary provision of alternative options for energy supply (emergency power supply).

capabilities.label: Provision of ICT/Telecom
capabilities.desc: Restoration or temporary supply of ICT and Telecommunication.

capabilities.label: Provide sanitation
capabilities.desc: Restore sanitation or provide temporary solutions.

capabilities.label: Command, Control and Coordination
capabilities.desc: Decision-making, planning and tasking activities at the various coordination and command levels that are involved in managing a certain disaster event (including volunteer management and collaboration with third parties while responding to the disaster situation).

capabilities.label: Situation assessment
capabilities.desc: Development of operational information through enrichment of collected data, including the development of a Common Operational Picture and Threat assessment.

capabilities.label: Information management
capabilities.desc: Storing (log-keeping) and sharing of information such as collected data, assessments made and decisions taken.

capabilities.label: Monitoring/Data collection
capabilities.desc: Collection of data by Physical monitoring (surveillance) and Data-mining.

capabilities.label: On-site operations support
capabilities.desc: Supply of basic services to first responders on-site or nearby the location of the incident to enable their response activities. It concerns Provision of Communication/ICT to first responders, Provision of Energy to first responders, and Guarantee Safety and Security to first responders.

capabilities.label: Logistics
capabilities.desc: Transport of personnel and materiel, including supply of relief goods (vaccines, food, tents, etc.), fuel and spare parts, to support sustained disaster response operations.

capabilities.label: Provide public health & safety
capabilities.desc: Provision of public health and safety services for those displaced. This covers for example physical impacts (including individuals’ health, housing and financial needs), psychological impacts, and deaths.

capabilities.label: Provide food and shelter
capabilities.desc: Provision of food and shelter for those displaced.

capabilities.label: Waste management
capabilities.desc: Clearance of large-scale pollution and decontamination, and dealing with waste.

capabilities.label: Restoration habitats
capabilities.desc: Restoration of natural resources and habitats.

items.placeholder: Prevent disasters / crisis situations.

items.label: Minimise losses from hazards
items.placeholder: Reduce / avoid losses from hazards.

items.label: Efficiency
items.placeholder: Relation between costs and results

capabilities.label: Business recovery
capabilities.desc: Business recovery (e.g. shops, industry that suffered from the disaster or crisis).

capabilities.label: Financial recovery
capabilities.desc: Recovery from financial impact on authorities.

capabilities.label: Re-establish transport
capabilities.desc: Re-establishment of transport routes (road, rail, water, air, pipelines).

capabilities.label: Re-establish utilities
capabilities.desc: Restoration of interrupted utilities and other essential services.

capabilities.label: Establish recovery organisation
capabilities.desc: •Establishment of recovery organisation structure. One or more recovery organisation structure(s) has/have to be established both on the short-term as well as on the long-term. Recovery structures and processes have to be established, based on the general organisation structures that are developed in the preparedness phase.

capabilities.label: Recovery programming
capabilities.desc: Determination and implementation of recovery programme. Based on an impact assessment a recovery programme has to be established on policy level, and has to be implemented in accordance to policy-decisions.

mainTasks.label: Help victims
mainTasks.desc: Assure prompt effective assistance to victims

performanceAspects.label: Effectiveness
performanceAspects.desc:
- Quality of the results
- Timeliness
- Endurance

performanceAspects.label: Safety/Security of professionals
performanceAspects.desc: Physical and mental safety of involved personnel

mainGaps.label: Technological
mainGaps.desc: E.g., Sensing Technologies, Command and control, communications and coordination, Surveillance, General public protection, Simulations, Physical protection, Crisis logistics, Technical Interoperability, ICT

lexicon.label: Process to determine the extent to which a capability is adequately performed and to determine the urgency to strengthen the capability

The means to accomplish one or more tasks under specific conditions. capabilities can be described along three types of resources: Technologies, Skills; competences of staff and Organization's work processes and procedures
lexicon.ref: [Project Responder 5, Homeland Security, Science and Technology, August 2017]

lexicon.label: The gap between the current ability to provide a response and the actual response assessed to be required for a given threat or hazard. Plans should be made to reduce or eliminate this gap, if the risk justifies it.
lexicon.ref: [Cabinet Office, UK Civil Protection Lexicon, version 2.1.1, February 2013]

lexicon.label: The process by which people, organisations and society systematically stimulate and develop their capacities over time to achieve social and economic goals, including through improvement of knowledge, skills, systems, and institutions.

A method to examine whether the performance of the current implementation of the capability meets the desired level (or ambition), and if not, to determine which steps need to be taken to improve the performance leve
The ability of diverse systems and organisations to work together, i.e. to interoperate.
Measures taken to prevent, limit and reduce impact of the negative consequences of incidents, emergencies and disasters.
Determine the status of an environment to alert personnel (i.e. emergency services) to the presence of an incident and to allow control (response) actions to be initiated.

Which aims at coordinating processes in which different organisations achieve a previously agreed and mutually beneficial goal. It focuses on integrating business processes and meeting end-user requirements by making services available, easily identifiable, accessible and user-focused.
Activities taken in order to prepare incident (disaster) response.
Restoration and improvement, where appropriate, facilities, livelihoods and living conditions of disaster-affected communities, including efforts to reduce risk factors.
Actions taken in order to stop the causes for the imminent hazard and to mitigate the consequences of potentially destabilizing or disruptive events and to recover to a normal situation.
Which is related to the technical issues involved in linking computer systems and services.
Potential cause of an unwanted incident, which may result in harm to individuals, a system or organisation, the environment or the community.
